violations.text: The chlorine concentration in the sanitizing solution of the 3-bay sink was 25 parts per million (ppm). The concentration of chlorine in the sanitizing solution must be between 50-100 ppm. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/27/2025

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. -Establishment shall register an employee with RIDOH Center for Food Protection as the Certified Food Safety Manager (RI license). Repeat Violation. Correct By: 11/30/2023

The chlorine concentration in the sanitizing solution of the 3-bay sink was (0) parts per million (ppm). The concentration of chlorine in the sanitizing solution must be between 50-100 ppm. -Staff added more chlorine to the solution. The concentration of the sanitizing solution observed at 50-100ppm. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/17/2023

ROP lobster meat observed in reduced oxygen environment and completely thawed out in the the walk-in cooler. ROP lobster meat observed at 37 degrees F. Label says, "Maintain Frozen". -Proper thawing of ROP seafood products reviewed. Staff removed the lobster meat from reduced oxygen packaging during this inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/17/2023
